weeks.weeklyGoal: Participants will understand diabetes, recognize the risks to young adults, and appreciate the importance of prevention.
learningOutcomes: Distinguish between Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes.
Explain why university students may be vulnerable.

imagePlaceholder.alt: Welcome to DiaShield Dashboard overview illustration

paragraphs: Welcome to the Diabetes Prevention Program. Over the next 12 weeks, you will learn how simple lifestyle habits can help prevent Type 2 diabetes.
Many people think diabetes only affects older adults, but unhealthy eating habits, physical inactivity, poor sleep, stress, and weight gain can increase risk even among young adults.

days.dailyChallenge: Log into the app twice today.
days.pushNotification: Welcome! Your journey toward diabetes prevention begins today.

imagePlaceholder.alt: Infographic breaking down differences between Type 1, Type 2, and Gestational Diabetes

paragraphs: There are several forms of diabetes. Type 1 is an autoimmune condition where the body stops producing insulin. Type 2 is the most common form where the body becomes resistant to insulin and is strongly linked to lifestyle.

days.dailyChallenge: Share one difference between Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes.
days.pushNotification: Most diabetes cases worldwide are Type 2 and many are preventable.

imagePlaceholder.alt: Visual checklist of key symptoms like excessive thirst and fatigue

paragraphs: Common symptoms include excessive thirst, frequent urination, excessive hunger, unexplained weight loss, fatigue, and blurred vision.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a common symptom of diabetes?
questions.explanation: High blood glucose levels can cause the body to lose more water, leading to increased thirst.

days.dailyChallenge: Discuss diabetes symptoms with a family member.
days.pushNotification: Knowing the signs can help prevent complications.
